expect: PHIL-16 contains 4 sub-claims, >=2 unfalsifiable in current architecture
actual: PHIL-16 contains 5 sub-claims (L-1352 prior art). 2 GROUNDED (self-improving, effective), 1 CONTESTED (good — benefit_ratio 2.03x but self-assessed), 2 FALSIFIED (helpful beyond itself, benefit-of-more — 0 external beneficiaries). Implemented decomposition: PHIL-16a (grounded, measured) + PHIL-16b (aspirational, deadline S600). Motte-and-bailey pattern: grounded sub-claims shield aspirational ones from challenge. PHILOSOPHY.md v1.5->v1.6. Challenge table updated. Dissolution deadline accelerated S700->S600.
diff: Expected >=2 unfalsifiable: found 3 aspirational (good, helpful, benefit-of-more), 2 falsified outright. 5 sub-claims not 4. Prior art L-1352 already diagnosed this in S507 — 2 sessions of diagnosis-without-repair confirms the pattern it describes.
